codeReview.summary: Embeddings and moderations match the requested compatibility behavior, but the models surface still rewrites caller-supplied ids before building retrieve/delete paths.

issues.description: `validate_path_id` trims nonblank model ids before `retrieve` and `delete` format the request path. That means inputs like `" model-owned "` are silently rewritten to `model-owned` instead of being preserved byte-for-byte, which violates the raw-id preservation required by VAL-COMPAT-007 and can target the wrong model on retrieve/delete. The current tests only cover blank ids, so this regression is not exercised.

summary: Reviewed the handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, and added tests for commit 355a68e. Result: fail, because `models.retrieve`/`models.delete` trim surrounding whitespace from nonblank ids instead of forwarding the exact caller-supplied id; no additional shared-state gaps were identified.
